chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated About Those Who Refuse to Pay Zakat

text: رِوَايَةِ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ مَنَعَ قِيرَاطاً مِنَ اَلزَّكَاةِ فَلَيْسَ بِمُؤْمِنٍ وَ لاَ مُسْلِمٍ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ : «۝ حَتّٰى إِذٰا جٰاءَ أَحَدَهُمُ اَلْمَوْتُ قٰالَ رَبِّ اِرْجِعُونِ. `لَعَلِّي أَعْمَلُ صٰالِحاً فِيمٰا تَرَكْتُ ۝» » وَ فِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى «وَ لاَ تُقْبَلُ لَهُ صَلاَةٌ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1591 - In a narration from Abu Basir, reported from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Whoever withholds even a carat of Zakat is neither a believer nor a Muslim. This is the meaning of Allah’s (swt) statement:
'Until, when death comes to one of them, he says, "My Lord (azj), send me back, that I might do righteousness in that which I left behind"' (Surah Al-Mu’minun 23:99-100)."
In another narration, it is stated: "And none of his prayers will be accepted."
